Round 6 complete. All 51 tests pass (45 prior + 6 new), clippy clean, `Whole`-side output byte-identical to Round 5. Since REPORT.md creation is guarded, the report is returned below; behavioral facts are persisted in BEHAVIOR.md §14 and QUERIES.log (round-6 block).

UNIT D ROUND 6 — LEMMA-VERDICT TAXONOMY COMPLETED

TAXONOMY (exhausted via oracle probing; every line traces to a logged capture)

Regular lemma line `  <name> (<kind>): <verdict> (<N> steps)` — verdict set is exactly four:
- `verified` (uniform for all-traces and exists-trace)
- `falsified - found trace` (all-traces)
- `falsified - no trace found` (exists-trace)
- `analysis incomplete` (unselected / `--prove` non-match / `--bound` cutoff)
No new phrase appears under `--stop-on-trace=SORRY|NONE` [r6_sot_*], for a `[sources]`-attributed lemma [r6_partialdecon — renders identically to a plain lemma], for open-chain/partial-deconstruction theories [r6_openchains_default, r6_woolam_noautosrc — no extra summary line], or on reloading embedded proofs [r6_analysed_reload — only verified/falsified].

NEW: `--diff` adds three prefixed line forms (each keeps the `  ` body indent):
- `  RHS :  <name> (<kind>): <verdict> (<N> steps)`  (projected ordinary lemma)
- `  LHS :  <name> (<kind>): <verdict> (<N> steps)`  (projected ordinary lemma)
- `  DiffLemma:  <name> : <verdict> (<N> steps)`  (observational-equivalence lemma: NO trace-kind; `:` with a space each side; falsified always `- found trace`; name always `Observational_equivalence`)
Byte detail: `RHS`/`LHS` use ` :  ` (space-colon-two-spaces); `DiffLemma` uses `:  ` (no space before colon).

DIFF ORDERING [r6_diff_with_lemma, r6_diff_two_lemmas, r6_diff_warn]: per ordinary lemma its RHS line then LHS line (grouped per lemma: RHS l1, LHS l1, RHS l2, LHS l2), then the single DiffLemma line last. The §12a body structure is unchanged: opening `  ` line, warning section first (with the `--prove` advisory), `  ` separator, then this lemma section. An inconsistent/unmirrored left rule is a wellformedness WARNING (existing count-line slot), not a new line form [r6_diff_lrmismatch].

NON-VERDICTS: lemma-level errors never produce a summary verdict line — a malformed lemma feeds the WARNING count-line slot; a solver/oracle failure aborts the whole run after `Theory closed` (`tamarin-prover: ./oracle: readCreateProcess: posix_spawnp: does not exist ...`, exit 1, empty stdout) [r6_oracle_err] — that is §8c runtime-error surface.

DIFF STDERR: engine-timing-driven (bare `diffLemma` marker after the preamble; `[Saturating Sources]`/`[Open Chains]` interleave WITHIN the five phases, unlike §10a). Left opaque and NOT byte-modelled; the diff fixtures assert only the STDOUT summary section via `render_summary`.

CODE CHANGES
- /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/console/workspace/cli-clean/src/framing.rs — added `enum LemmaSide { Whole, Rhs, Lhs, Diff }` and a `side` field on `LemmaOutcome`; extracted shared `verdict_phrase(result, kind)`; added constructors `LemmaOutcome::whole` / `::projected` / `::diff_lemma`; rewrote `line()` to render the RHS/LHS/DiffLemma forms. `Whole` output is byte-identical to Round 5 (kept `Summary.lemmas` a single ordered Vec, so no `Summary` literal churn).
- /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/console/workspace/cli-clean/src/lib.rs — export `LemmaSide`.
- /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/console/workspace/cli-clean/tests/cli_tests.rs — imported `LemmaSide`; rewrote the `lemma` helper onto `LemmaOutcome::whole`; added 6 tests (`diff_summary_regular_lemma_then_obs_equiv_verified`, `diff_summary_two_lemmas_grouped_rhs_then_lhs_per_lemma`, `diff_summary_warning_precedes_diff_lines_under_prove`, `diff_summary_obs_equiv_incomplete_only`, `diff_summary_obs_equiv_falsified_found_trace`, `diff_summary_line_bytes_are_exact`).
- New fixtures (captured program output): /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/console/workspace/cli-clean/tests/fixtures/r6_diff_{with_lemma,two_lemmas,warn,default,n5n6}.out.txt
- New probe theories (self-authored): /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/console/workspace/probes/round6/{diff_equiv_true,diff_with_lemma,diff_two_lemmas,diff_warn,partialdecon,open_chains}.spthy
- Captures: /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/console/workspace/captures/r6_*.{out,err}.txt
- Docs: BEHAVIOR.md §14 (14a–14f) and QUERIES.log round-6 block updated with provenance.

VERIFICATION: `cargo test` = 51 passed / 0 failed (was 45); `cargo clippy --all-targets` = no warnings; `cargo build` clean. All prior fixtures/tests unchanged and green.

10-LINE SUMMARY
1. The reference's per-lemma summary lines had more forms than the prior 3-verdict model.
2. Regular lemmas have exactly 4 verdict phrases (verified / found-trace falsified / no-trace-found falsified / analysis incomplete); nothing else surfaces a new phrase.
3. `--diff` introduces `RHS :  ` and `LHS :  ` prefixes on projected ordinary-lemma lines.
4. `--diff` reports the auto lemma as `DiffLemma:  Observational_equivalence : <verdict>`, with no `(kind)` and falsified reading `- found trace`.
5. Diff lines are ordered RHS/LHS per lemma with DiffLemma last; the warning section still precedes the lemma section.
6. `[sources]` lemmas and partial deconstructions add no summary line form.
7. `--stop-on-trace` variants and embedded-proof reloads add no new verdict phrase.
8. Lemma-level errors are not summary verdicts (they hit the WARNING slot or abort the whole run).
9. `LemmaOutcome` gained a `side` field via `LemmaSide`; `Whole` output is byte-identical to Round 5.
10. 6 new byte-parity tests + 5 captured fixtures; 51/51 tests pass with a clean build.

Round 6 audit complete. Appended the round section to /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/console/AUDIT.md.

Delta audited (both-sides): the `--diff` lemma-verdict taxonomy in console unit D — `framing.rs` (new `LemmaSide` enum + `whole`/`projected`/`diff_lemma` constructors, `verdict_phrase` free-function extraction, `RHS :  `/`LHS :  `/`DiffLemma:  ` line forms), `lib.rs` re-export, `cli_tests.rs`, `BEHAVIOR.md` §14, `QUERIES.log` Round-6 block, and the `probes/round6/*` + `r6_diff_*` fixtures.

Findings, all filtered clean:
- Interop line forms (`RHS :  `, `LHS :  `, `DiffLemma:  <name> : <verdict> (<N> steps)`, the verdict phrases, `Observational_equivalence`) are byte-exact boundary constants, each directly captured and logged (QUERIES.log 00:40:00–00:49:00; BEHAVIOR.md §14b) — merger/probe-derived.
- Structure diverges from the GPL source, not copies it: upstream `prettyClosedDiffSummary` uses two list-comprehensions (`lemmaSummaries` over `EitherLemmaItem`, `diffLemmaSummaries` over `DiffLemmaItem`) joined `$$`, plus a separate `showDiffProofStatus`. The clean collapses to one `LemmaOutcome` tagged by `side` and reuses its own 3-value `verdict_phrase(result, AllTraces)` — omitting the three un-probed upstream statuses, the fingerprint of observed-only reconstruction.
- No source identifier carried (`EitherLemmaItem`/`DiffLemmaItem`/`prettyClosedDiffSummary`/`lDiffName`/`Side`); `Rhs`/`Lhs` are idiomatic casing of the output token, `Whole`/`Diff` have no upstream analogue.
- No comment lineage — upstream's `Just`-invariant note and `TODO … hacky construction` block are not echoed.
- Closure probes (sources/open-chains, stop-on-trace, oracle-error, analysed-reload) added zero new source, each logging a negative taxonomy result.
- Probes are self-authored; `r6_diff_*.out.txt` fixtures are the reference program's own golden bytes.

No violations; no behavioral redo instructions needed.

VERDICT: pass

All green: lib builds clean, 104 tests pass across all 8 binaries, clippy clean on all targets, and only files under my cluster were modified. STEP 1 and STEP 2 complete; my servers are stopped.

---

UNIT A round 7 (concurrency-safe dispatch) — COMPLETE. Clean-room protocol respected: no file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/ was read; all behavior traces to logged live probes.

STEP 1 — probed the reference's concurrency contract (live, ports 3100/3101; long op = the sapic/slow PKCS11 lemma cannot_obtain_key_ind, ~30s, terminating). Logged as QUERIES.log [R70]–[R76] and written up as BEHAVIOR.md §17:
- Fully NON-BLOCKING [R71]: with the ~30s autoprove in flight, a concurrent burst (reads on other theories, reads on the SAME theory being proved, reads of other versions, a 2nd proof op on a different theory, a 2nd proof op on the SAME theory, an upload POST /, and a reload incl. same base index) all returned in 0.02–0.52s, long before the long op finished. Nothing blocked.
- Allocation is at COMMIT, not request-start [R72]: the long op STARTED first (t=0.014s) but COMMITTED last (t=30.57s) and got the HIGHEST index (10); the fast ops that committed at ~2.6s got the lower indices 8/9. Indices follow completion order.
- Version INVISIBLE until commit [R73]: index-page polling never showed the long op's index until after completion.
- Atomic under races [R74]: 12 truly-simultaneous proof ops → indices 12..23, distinct/contiguous, zero collision, zero skip.
- Snapshot isolation [R75]: a long autoprove on idx3 completed fine (→ idx24) even though idx3 was reloaded in place mid-compute (the reload itself served concurrently); retention reconfirmed (capped-out idx4 still 200).
- Incidental [R76]: autoprove can fail with {"alert":"Sorry, but the autoprover () failed!"} — logged as a documented gap, out of round-7 scope.

STEP 2 — redesigned dispatch to honor that contract, in /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/weblayer/workspace/web-clean/src/dispatch.rs:
- Server::dispatch now takes &self (one server shared across concurrent requests). Every request runs get-snapshot → compute → commit: resolve+take one cheap OWNED snapshot via StateOps::snapshot (state lock released), run the ProverOps call — including the slow ones (autoprove*, apply_method, del_*, reload, load_theory, edits) — on the snapshot with NO state lock held, then commit atomically (insert_new allocates the fresh monotonic index at commit; replace for in-place reload/edits). The only serialized sections are the microsecond atomic StateOps calls. Documented design choice: a single dispatch entry point rather than a static cheap/slow route split, because the pipeline already isolates the lock-free compute from the two tiny atomic state calls (route classification would be a lossy proxy for "hold no lock during any ProverOps call"). A side benefit: since compute runs outside the lock, a prover panic cannot poison state.
- StateOps reshaped to an interior-mutability, snapshot-handing trait (all &self; get→snapshot returns an owned copy; entries→indices + snapshot). Reference InMemoryState<T: Clone> is now Mutex<BTreeMap + counter>, holding the lock only for each map/counter op. Server + InMemoryState compose to Send+Sync so a consumer wraps the server in Arc and serves from any async task; a real backend implements the same &self façade over its own async cache.

Tests (all byte-parity tests stay green):
- New /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/weblayer/workspace/web-clean/tests/dispatch7.rs (2 tests) with a controllable GatedProver whose autoprove parks mid-compute. slow_op_in_flight_is_non_blocking_and_commits_last asserts the exact probed interleaving (a read is served while the op is parked; a fast op commits first → index 2; the parked op's index is invisible/unresolvable pre-commit; on release it commits last → index 3; final set contiguous). concurrent_allocations_never_collide_or_skip races 16 threads → a contiguous unique index block ([R74]). Verified non-flaky over 20 consecutive runs; the test structurally requires &self dispatch (it would deadlock if any lock were held across compute, and wouldn't compile under &mut self).
- Updated tests/dispatch6.rs (WrapState custom backend + InMemoryState contract tests to the &self snapshot API), tests/dispatch5.rs (body helper → &Server), and dispatch.rs/dispatch4.rs (let mut s → let s).

Results: cargo test → 104 passing (26 unit + 15 dispatch + 17 dispatch4 + 14 dispatch5 + 7 dispatch6 + 2 dispatch7 + 23 parity); cargo clippy --all-targets clean; lib builds clean.

Docs updated: BEHAVIOR.md §17 (+ a §16.2 superseding note), QUERIES.log [R70]–[R76], REPORT.md round-7 section. Probe scripts committed under /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/weblayer/workspace/scratch/ (wl_server7.sh, r7/probe_concurrency.sh, r7/probe_long.sh, r7/probe_isolation.sh).

Servers: my probe servers on 3100 and 3101 are stopped (no 31xx listeners, no wl7 pid files). NOTE for the orchestrator: an unrelated tamarin-prover interactive server is running on port 3200 (pid 1229593, workdir /tmp/tmp.NzPRbNTlT8) — it is outside my sanctioned 3100–3199 range and was NOT started by me, so I left it untouched; it likely belongs to another unit/session.

Round 7 both-sides similarity audit of weblayer complete. Section appended to `/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/weblayer/AUDIT.md`.

Delta audited (vs clean-room HEAD e76455a, restricted to `weblayer/`): `src/dispatch.rs` (substantive), new `tests/dispatch7.rs`, mechanical adaptations in `tests/dispatch{,4,5,6}.rs`, plus `BEHAVIOR.md` §17, `QUERIES.log` [R70]–[R76], `REPORT.md`. Compared against `src/Web/Handler.hs`, `src/Web/Types.hs`, `src/Web/Settings.hs`.

The round is a behaviour-neutral concurrency refactor: `StateOps` becomes an interior-mutability, snapshot-handing trait (`get`→`snapshot` owned clone, `entries`→`indices`, all `&self`); `InMemoryState` wraps `Mutex<BTreeMap+counter>`; `Server::dispatch(&self)` runs get-snapshot → compute → commit.

Filtration outcome:
- The three-phase snapshot→compute→commit shape is merger/scenes-à-faire — the single correct concurrent implementation given the probed constraints ([R71]/[R72]/[R74]/[R75]). Upstream converges on it only because Haskell immutability makes the snapshot free; the clean side must clone explicitly (`T: Clone`) — a Rust-necessity divergence, not copying.
- Monotonic allocation is expressed via a stored `next_index` counter that affirmatively diverges from upstream's stateless `findMax + 1` (behaviourally distinguishable on delete-top-then-insert).
- `Mutex<BTreeMap>` vs `MVar (Map …)`: convergent idiom, no structural or identifier overlap.
- Identifier constellation: zero overlap with upstream's state/thread vocabulary (grep of full delta for `theoryVar`/`getTheory`/`putTheory`/`modifyMVar`/`findMax`/`evalInThread`/etc. returns nothing).
- Upstream's most distinctive concurrency expression — the `threadVar`/`evalInThread`/`putThread`/`killThread` cancellable-thread subsystem — is affirmatively NOT reproduced.
- No comment lineage; provenance genuinely probe-derived (§17 + [R70]–[R76] with concrete timings).

One non-blocking hygiene note (not verdict-bearing): shipped doc comments in `dispatch.rs` gained inline `BEHAVIOR.md §17.x` / `([R71])` provenance citations and "probed"-narration, extending the Round-6 comments-current-state-only regression note. This is the clean-room's own narration (opposite of copying), so no similarity impact and no redo.

Findings surviving filtration: 0. No redo instructions issued.

VERDICT: pass

ROUND 7 (web dispatch) — concurrency-safe dispatch: VERIFIED COMPLETE.

WHAT I FOUND. This was a resume: the round-7 snapshot→compute→commit redesign was already fully implemented in the working tree (uncommitted, from a prior ~01:00 session) — dispatch.rs, dispatch7.rs, BEHAVIOR §17, and the REPORT round-7 section all present and mutually consistent. I did NOT redo it from scratch (that would have destroyed good work); I verified it is genuine and correct, and independently re-probed the reference to satisfy STEP 1 with my own hands rather than trusting the prior logs.

STEP 1 — live re-probe of the reference contract (new: QUERIES.log [R77]). Ran tamarin-prover interactive on the sanctioned multi-theory dir (idx1=Tutorial, 2=NSLPK3, 3=NAXOS_eCK, 4=RYY) on port 3100; server stopped afterward. Corroborated all three core semantics:
- Non-blocking (§17.1): during an in-flight RYY autoprove, a 5-way concurrent burst all returned 200 in 0.02–0.34s — read of another theory, read of the SAME theory being proved, read of another version, a fast proof op, and an upload. A read fired mid-compute of a ~1.5s NAXOS autoprove also returned 200 in 0.02s.
- Commit-order allocation (§17.2): the RYY autoprove STARTED first (t=0.004) but COMMITTED last (t=0.634) and got the HIGHER index 6; a fast Tutorial method committed at t=0.427 and got the LOWER index 5. Indices follow completion order.
- Invisible-until-commit (§17.2/§17.3): PRE max=7; a ~1.5s NAXOS autoprove (start t=0.017, commit t=1.536 → idx8) was absent from every version poll across its compute window (t=0.032…1.346 all showed [1..7]) and appeared only at the first post-commit poll (t=1.564). Contiguous, no skip.

STEP 2 — the redesign (already in place, confirmed correct). Server::dispatch takes &self (shared across concurrent requests). StateOps is an interior-mutability &self trait (snapshot/indices; insert_new/replace/remove); InMemoryState<T> is a Mutex<BTreeMap+counter> holding the lock only for each map/counter op. Every handler runs get-snapshot → lock-free ProverOps compute → atomic commit; no state lock is held across any ProverOps call. The controllable-fake interleaving test exists (dispatch7.rs: GatedProver whose autoprove parks on a Condvar gate) and asserts exactly the probed interleaving (non-blocking read while parked; fast op commits first→idx2; parked op's index invisible/unresolvable pre-commit; on release commits last→idx3; final set contiguous), plus a 16-thread allocation race asserting a contiguous unique block.

VERIFICATION. cargo test → 104 passing (26 unit + 15 dispatch + 17 dispatch4 + 14 dispatch5 + 7 dispatch6 + 2 dispatch7 + 23 parity); cargo clippy --tests --examples clean (rc 0); dispatch7 non-flaky over 30 consecutive runs; all byte-parity tests green.

HONEST GAPS (documented, not defects). (1) Two concurrent in-place edits/reloads on the SAME index is unprobed; the impl's last-writer-computed-against-its-start-snapshot is consistent with the observed snapshot-isolation model (§17.4) but that exact race wasn't probed. (2) autoprove-failure alert branch ([R76]) is modeled as always-redirect — an out-of-scope documented gap.

Here is the plain-text round summary.

UNIT D — round 6 (cont.): lemma-verdict taxonomy completed and verified.

STARTING STATE. The crate's `LemmaResult` enum has 3 variants (Verified / Falsified / AnalysisIncomplete); `verdict_phrase(result, kind)` already expands these into 4 whole-lemma phrases, and a `LemmaSide` field (Whole/Rhs/Lhs/Diff) already renders the `--diff` prefixed forms. BEHAVIOR §14 and the r6_diff_* captures/tests existed. Baseline: 51 tests green.

THE GAP I CLOSED. Every prior `--diff` capture exercised the projected RHS/LHS lines only as `verified`, so the falsified/incomplete projected forms were code-derived but never observed against the reference. I drove them out with self-authored probe theories (probes/round6/{diff_false,diff_asym,two_lemma}.spthy) via the split-stream harness:

- Projected FALSIFIED, both kinds [r6_diff_false, --diff --prove]: `RHS/LHS :  leaked (all-traces): falsified - found trace (3 steps)` and `RHS/LHS :  impossible (exists-trace): falsified - no trace found (2 steps)`. Projected lines carry the same kind-dependent falsified phrase as whole-theory lemmas.
- Projected ANALYSIS INCOMPLETE [r6_diff_lemma_noprove, --diff without --prove]: `RHS/LHS :  sent_secret (all-traces): analysis incomplete (1 steps)` plus `DiffLemma:  ... : analysis incomplete (1 steps)`.
- Sides are INDEPENDENT, not mirrored [r6_diff_asym, diff(~m, senc(~m,~k))]: `RHS :  secret (all-traces): verified (3 steps)` vs `LHS :  secret (all-traces): falsified - found trace (3 steps)` in one theory. LHS = first diff() arg (left system), RHS = second (right system).

LEMMA SELECTION / "skipped" state [r6_twolemma_proveone, r6_twolemma_lemmafilter]: an unselected/filtered lemma is NEVER omitted from the summary — `--prove=first` (and `--lemma=first --prove`) still lists `second (exists-trace): analysis incomplete (1 steps)`. There is no distinct "skipped" line form. A `--prove`/`--lemma` prefix that matches no lemma is itself a wellformedness check ("... does not correspond to a specified lemma") and feeds the existing WARNING count-line slot (count 1 + advisory), reinforcing §14e.

ORDERING RE-VERIFIED [r6_multi_warn_two, warn+lemma theory then two-lemma theory, --prove]: within a theory the warning section (count + `--prove` advisory) precedes the `  ` separator and the lemma lines; lemmas print in declaration order; theories are blank-line separated; stderr is the preamble once then each theory's five phases. Matches frame_batch byte-for-byte.

The complete per-lemma summary-line taxonomy is now: 6 whole forms ({all-traces,exists-trace} × {verified, falsified-<kind-suffix>, analysis incomplete}), the same 6 under `RHS :  `/`LHS :  ` projection, and 3 `DiffLemma:  <name> : <verdict>` forms (no kind; falsified always `- found trace`) — all observed and byte-parity-tested. Lemma errors remain non-verdicts (§14e): whole-theory WARNING or whole-run runtime abort.

DELIVERABLES. No code change was needed — the existing render path already produced every newly-observed form correctly; the round converts inferred forms into observed, fixtured ones. Added 5 fixtures (tests/fixtures/r6_diff_false.out.txt, r6_diff_lemma_noprove.out.txt, r6_diff_asym.out.txt, r6_multi_warn_two.{out,err}.txt) and 4 byte-parity tests (diff_summary_projected_falsified_both_kinds, diff_summary_projected_incomplete_without_prove, diff_summary_projected_sides_are_independent, frame_batch_multi_warn_then_two_lemmas_reproduces_both_streams). Updated QUERIES.log (6 new probe entries) and BEHAVIOR.md §14a/§14b/§14f. Tests: 55 passed, 0 failed; clean build, no warnings. No REPORT.md exists; round record folded into BEHAVIOR.md §14 per the fallback.

Round 8 complete. All deliverables met.

UNIT B round 8 — RESULT: replaced the closed-form fill with a faithful HughesPJ layout engine and cracked the record-cell feeding. Wrapping-cell corpus byte-exactness rose from ~44% to **81.1%**; the Wide-rule probe is byte-exact for ALL cells; GRAPHCLEAN_CORPUS round-trip stays **12022/12022**. All servers stopped, ports 3200-3299 clear.

THE TWO FINDINGS THAT DID IT
1. **Ribbon = 1.5.** The 87 flat-fit boundary is HughesPJ's *ribbon*, not the line length. Rendering at ribbonsPerLine=1.5 (lineLength = ⌊3·budget/2⌋, i.e. 1.5× the ribbon) makes `fill` produce RAGGED paragraph fills — a physical line can be shorter than a later one. That reproduces corpus cells like `St_1_gNB( ~gNB_ID, KD8,` │ `KD1, '0', AM2, GN1` │ `)` (2 args, then 4 wider args) that no greedy fill (lineLength==ribbon, my prior model) can ever emit. This was the dominant residual: the closed-form/greedy fill was structurally incapable of ragged output. Derived by finding such cells in the corpus (band=[0,0] = unreproducible at any single width) then live-re-probing with `layout_at` LINELEN/RIBBON.
2. **Per-group share = proportional.** Each prem/concl cell gets fit budget `B_i = max(round(87·flat_i/T), 20)`, T = Σ flats. Lone cell (T=flat) → 87. Best of every allocator on the full census (proportional 81.1% vs smallest-first 62.6%, flat-sum 59.3%, prop-ceil 79.9%, reserve-small 77.4%). Reproduces Wide byte-exact: Ack→21 (wraps), Big→57 (8 tuple elems), Out→9→20 (fits). The engine at the right budget is byte-exact, so single-cell wrapping cells (budget always 87) hit **94.7%** — the near-ceiling that proves the cell doc + engine are faithful.

RESIDUAL (honest, ~19% of wrapping cells): (a) ±1 `fits` boundary where proportional lands a column off the reference's own coupled per-cell fits (e.g. `In_S( 'D2', 'H2', spkDD )`: prop=24.2→24 keeps `spkDD` on line 0, reference breaks at ≤23); (b) cells wrapped on their UN-abbreviated width (abbreviations substituted post-layout — outside a crate that receives post-abbreviation text); (c) `++`-union / deep function-application internal breaks the fact/tuple grammar doesn't model. All need the reference's exact per-cell fits over the pre-abbreviation document.
